description: Update CDK infrastructure to grant the Lambda function S3 read access on the input bucket (currently only has write on output bucket and DynamoDB access), add INPUT_BUCKET_NAME environment variable, and update cdk-base_test.go to verify the new permissions and env var. Also update ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect the now-functional audio processing pipeline with detailed processing steps and output handling. Delete testdata/snapshot.json since the CDK template will change.
steps: Step 1 - Write failing tests FIRST in cdk-base_test.go: (a) test that Lambda has INPUT_BUCKET_NAME environment variable, (b) test that Lambda execution role has s3:GetObject permission on the input bucket (via GrantRead). Follow the existing test pattern using assertions.Template_FromStack and HasResourceProperties.

Step 2 - Update cdk-base.go to: (a) grant the processorLambda read access to the inputBucket using inputBucket.GrantRead(processorLambda, nil), (b) add INPUT_BUCKET_NAME environment variable to the Lambda's Environment map referencing inputBucket.BucketName().
Step 3 - Delete testdata/snapshot.json since the CDK template has changed (the snapshot test will regenerate it on next run).

Step 4 - Update ARCHITECTURE.md: (a) Update the 'Processing Layer' section to describe the full audio processing flow (download -> Polly synthesis -> upload -> DynamoDB update), (b) Update the Lambda's documented permissions to include S3 read on input bucket, (c) Add a new subsection 'Audio Processing Flow' describing the step-by-step processing, (d) Update the Mermaid 'Current State Diagram' to show the Lambda actually reading from S3 input bucket and writing to S3 output bucket (remove the 'future:' annotation on the S3Out connection), (e) Add output file naming convention (processed/{audioId}/{timestamp}.mp3), (f) Document the expanded Response struct fields.
Step 5 - Ensure the CDK code compiles syntactically (review for correctness since go build cannot run for the root module).
acceptance_criteria: cdk-base_test.go includes test for INPUT_BUCKET_NAME environment variable on the Lambda
cdk-base_test.go includes test for S3 read permission (s3:GetObject) on the Lambda execution role for the input bucket
cdk-base.go grants inputBucket read access to processorLambda and adds INPUT_BUCKET_NAME to Lambda environment
ARCHITECTURE.md accurately describes the full processing pipeline including download, Polly synthesis, upload, and DynamoDB update
ARCHITECTURE.md Mermaid diagram shows Lambda reading from S3 input and writing to S3 output (no 'future:' annotation)
verification: Verify cdk-base.go contains inputBucket.GrantRead and INPUT_BUCKET_NAME in the environment map
Verify cdk-base_test.go contains new test functions for INPUT_BUCKET_NAME and S3 read permissions
Verify ARCHITECTURE.md contains updated processing flow documentation
findings: CDK root module cannot be built/tested due to storage.googleapis.com being blocked. All changes verified via code review and syntax correctness. Lambda processor module (lambda/processor) still builds successfully. Tests follow the exact same pattern as TestLambdaFunctionHasOutputBucketEnv and TestLambdaHasOutputBucketWritePermission. GrantRead on S3 bucket in CDK generates s3:GetObject, s3:GetBucketLocation, and s3:ListBucket permissions.
